# **INTERNSHIP COMPLETION LETTER** {#internship-completion-letter}

**![][image2]**

# **PREFACE**

 First of all I want to thank God for giving me the strength to complete my internship. I feel His guidance helped me through every step.   
  I still have a lot to learn. I also want to thank my parents for being there for me. They supported me in ways. I will also not forget to thank Miss Siti Nur Ubed Ubaedillah, my internship supervisor as well as IT Head. She’s the one i usually bother with things related to administration and my Intern needs. I want to thank Pak Gafar, the CEO for letting me inter at PT. Shibly Teknologi Solusi. I also want to thank my coworkers and friends i made along the way at Shibly. They shared their valuable experiences and knowledges with me.  
   During my time at Shibly Solution, I learned many practical skills and Experiences  that I could not learn in class. I saw how teams communicate, how tasks are divided, how projects are managed, and how Developers work together to solve problems. This made me understand that working as Developer is not only about technical knowledge, but also about teamwork, communication skills to be able to interact with clients and coworker without misunderstanding each other,. 

Cikarang, June 2026   
João Zinho Franklin Saldanh

# 

# **CHAPTER Ⅰ** {#chapter-ⅰ}

# **INTRODUCTION** {#introduction}

1. ## **Internship Background**

The internship is an essential aspect of the academic curriculum that is meant to offer students hands-on experience in work settings. During this time, I have been placed at PT Shibly Technology Solusi. This is because this institution is crucial to the country's success in achieving modern digital and telecommunications advancements. This internship opportunity is expected to offer students the opportunity to apply various theories learned in class to practical situations, such as how enterprise systems are developed and regulated by an IT company.

2. ## **Internship Objectives**

The main goal of this internship is the attainment of a full understanding of the operations in a multinational technology company. I aim at the development of my professional skills in the areas of frontend development, UI implementation, and system integration. Moreover, this period will help in the acquisition of a full appreciation of the existing frameworks of enterprise systems and real-world project task management.

3. ## **Internship Benefits**

* ### **Internship Benefits for the Students**

The value of the internship to the student includes the rare chance to change their lives as a result of being introduced to the context of Tech work at multinational company. The internship will present opportunities to develop their professional identity as well as their connections with members of the governing body, experts, and the tech sector. The internship will also present an opportunity to identify the realms of interest in acquiring a special field of career as well as the "know-how" required to be more valuable in the job market both locally and internationally as a result of acquiring educational degrees.

* ### **Internship Benefits for the University**

For the university, this memorandum of understanding has been beneficial since it has helped create an institutional presence at the core technology institutions of Timor Leste and Indonesia. Through this relationship, the university gets to understand industry trends and evolving needs of the government in terms of technology, which are then used to enhance the current academic program to make it relevant and up to date. Being successful at placing students also helps to market the institution as a centre of excellence that can provide graduates who are ready to take a new nation to greater heights.

* ### **Internship Benefits for the Institution**

The presence of interns brings fresh academic perspectives, updated technical knowledge, and a high level of motivation to the assigned departments, meaning the company benefits greatly from their presence. Interns contribute to the support of daily administrative work and technical projects, allowing permanent staff to focus on higher levels of strategic planning while interns help them out with low level codings and help in basic works. Furthermore, provision for the internship programs allows the company to fulfil its social and national mandate by mentoring young professionals in ensuring a pipeline of qualified personnel so that a successive generation is prepared to lead the technology sectors for future to come

# **CHAPTER II COMPANY PROFILE**

1. ## **Institution Overview**

## STS is Indonesia's leading IT system integration and services provider, supporting customers in planning, building, and managing their IT infrastructure with a combination of strong technical competencies and comprehensive solutions. Over the years, the company has established a presence in three countries and has continuously expanded its capabilities to meet evolving market demand.

## 

2. ## **History of the Company** {#history-of-the-company}

    Shibly Teknologi Solusi (STS) started its journey as an IT solutions company with a strong focus on digital systems and technology development. One of its early milestones was its involvement in a major project with Ericsson in 2016, which helped the company build experience in handling large-scale technology projects.  
   In 2017, STS entered the Timor-Leste market, marking an important step in its regional expansion.      The company continued to grow in 2018 through its Digital Customer Services Project, which strengthened its reputation in providing practical and innovative digital solutions. In 2019, STS expanded its services into news, entertainment, and advertisement-related projects, showing its ability to support different types of industries.  
   The company’s growth continued in 2020 when it entered the Australian market, proving that its services could compete beyond the local and regional level. In 2021, STS also began exploring opportunities in the Middle East market as part of its long-term vision to become a global IT solutions provider.  
    Over the years, STS has been involved in many important systems, especially in the telecommunications and financial technology sectors. For Telkomcel Timor-Leste, STS developed several major systems, including the Core Online Charging System, Call Center system, MyTelkomcel application, TPAY e-wallet, and other internal systems.  
   STS has also supported banking and payment-related systems for BRI Timor-Leste, including BRIZZI and the TRX money transfer system, as well as several other internal systems used to support financial operations.  
   One of the company’s newest and largest projects is Project Saúde, which focuses on transforming paper-based health administration into digital systems for hospitals across Timor-Leste, which shows STS’s contribution.  
   STS has also worked with Timor Telecom, another major telecommunications company, by developing systems such as the Call Center system and other internal platforms, including Human Resources systems. In addition, STS has provided internal-use systems for ANATL, the airport authority in Timor-Leste.  
Beyond Timor-Leste, STS has also worked as a technology vendor for Telkomcel Indonesia and LinkAja. Its work includes developing and maintaining CRM systems for linkaja, as well as supporting new digital innovation projects. One of the newest projects is the development of AI features for the LinkAja application.

3. ## **Organization Chart** {#organization-chart}

Fig. 1 Organization Chart

## **4\. Vision, Mission, and Objectives**

**Vision:** To be one of the world's leading and innovative IT system integrations and services provider companies.

**Mission:** To provide the best quality service, cost effectiveness, and timely solutions through professional delivery of IT services.

**Objective:** To establish various business models based on turnkey and or manage services to meet a long-term and sustainable business.

## **5\. Core Organization Activities**

PT Shibly Technology Solution provides digital and telecommunication solutions, and offers various technology services, including:

* **CMS (Content Management System)** – Manages and organizes digital content efficiently.  
* **DMS (Document Management System)** – Stores and manages documents digitally.  
* **CRM (Customer Relationship Management)** – Enhances customer relationship management and communication.  
* **Captive Portal** – Authenticates users before accessing public Wi-Fi services.  
* **Cell Broadcast System** – Delivers mass messages to mobile users within a specific area.  
* **SMSC (Short Message Service Centre)** – Routes and manages SMS delivery.  
* **Charging Gateway** – Handles real-time billing and payment processing for telecommunication services.  
* **Online Charging System (OCS)** – Provides real-time customer billing based on usage.  
* **Value Added Services (VAS)** – Delivers additional telecommunication features such as messaging and digital content.  
* **Core Network Solutions** – Supports reliable telecommunication network infrastructure.  
* **Application Development** – Develops custom software solutions for business needs.  
* **Internet of Things (IoT)** – Connects devices for smart and automated operations.  
* **IT Consulting** – Provides technology advisory services to support digital transformation.

# **CHAPTER III INTERNSHIP ACTIVITIES**

* **Job Description**

During the internship, I worked as a Frontend Programmer Intern at PT Shibly. My responsibilities primarily focused on frontend development, including UI design, UI implementation, bug fixing, system testing, and support for client-based projects. I was involved in several enterprise and telecommunication systems, such as Telkomcel’s Helpdesk, CRM360, Online Charging System (OCS), Advice Monitor, Zone Module, and BRI-related web applications, serving mainly Telkomcel and BRI clients.

The responsibility also entailed applying a detail-oriented, user-centered approach to ensure accuracy in UI implementation, visual consistency, and efficient user interaction across these enterprise applications.

2. **Task and Responsibilities**

During my internship at PT Shibly, my tasks progressed from system orientation and UI evaluation to active frontend development, participation in client meetings, and involvement in User Acceptance Testing (UAT). I began by familiarizing myself with the company’s development environment and the Online Charging System (OCS) used by Telkomcel. Over the months, I actively developed and maintained frontend features, improving the UI/UX across multiple modules such as Zone Management and Advice Monitor. My role expanded to include supporting the development of an internal HR website alongside new team members, contributing to the Sinorita project through bug fixes, and participating in the Saude Hospital Information System for the Ministry of Health of Timor-Leste, where I implemented Portuguese and Tetum translations. Finally, I participated in a week-long UAT session with Telkomcel representatives to present features and receive direct client feedback.

* **UI Implementation & Optimization:** Reviewed existing frontend interfaces, identified UI defects and usability issues, and implemented interface improvements based on functional requirements and client feedback.  
* **Frontend Logic:** Enhanced UI functionality by implementing input validation logic, data filtering mechanisms, dashboard visualizations (charts), and UI animations to improve user interaction and system usability.  
* **API Integration:** Utilized API documentation using Swagger to consume backend services and integrate dynamic data into frontend components, particularly for dashboard and monitoring modules.  
* **System Testing & QA:** Supported User Acceptance Testing (UAT) activities, applied manual UI testing, and resolved frontend issues identified during testing phases.  
* **Communication & Reporting:** Actively participated in internal development meetings and client coordination meetings with Telkomcel, reporting development progress and discussing technical issues

##  {#heading}

# **CHAPTER IV EVALUATION** {#chapter-iv-evaluation}

1. ## **The Advantages of Internship**

Completing an internship at PT Shibly Teknologi Solusi opens new perspective for me of how an IT company operates, especially in project management and partnerships in Timor-Leste. The internship gave practical experience in business processes, programming, and real project tasks. It also gave me a better understanding of how the Real World operates. During the internship, there were lots of opportunities to meet and interact with professionals from different backgrounds. By joining discussions, reviewing projects, and supporting team activities, professional connections with Timor Telecom, Telkomcel, and BRI were expanded. This experience helped build workplace communication skills, collaboration with diverse teams, and gained lessons from experienced colleagues in this  field.

2. ## **Theory Implementation**

During this internship, I was able to implement my academic knowledge into real-world applications. Practical tasks such as implementing UI/UX improvements, developing data filtering mechanisms and dashboard visualizations, integrating APIs using Swagger, and applying professional standards with technologies like React and JavaScript demonstrated how theory is applied within enterprise systems. Key lessons I learned are understanding the role of frontend development in enterprise and telecommunication systems, learning how frontend systems interact with backend services through APIs, and understanding how frontend development directly supports business operations, customer service, and decision-making processes.

3. ## **Technical Skills and Knowledge**

At the beginning of the internship, I realized that my preparation in several technical areas was limited, particularly in using specific frontend frameworks and libraries such as React and Tailwind, which were completely new to me. I also needed time to fully understand the architecture of enterprise-level systems. Because not all tasks and concepts are explained in a step-by-step manner, I had to take the initiative to study unfamiliar technologies independently, particularly React and TypeScript, from the fundamentals. This self-learning enabled me to understand new technologies more efficiently. Ultimately, I gained experience in developing and maintaining UI components, improved my skills in Coding more Efficiently, and improved my debugging skills by encountering errors and testing skills and receiving feedback from Coworkers from QA and real experience in UAT with real clients.

4. ## **Personal Development**

Working alongside experienced colleagues required efficient time management, and timely completion of assignments. Balancing multiple responsibilities, adapting to new tasks, and working independently when necessary—especially as some projects like Call Center which were urged by the client, requiring a faster pace of work and sometimes needing us to work overtime at night. In work I usually participate in discussions, asking coworkers about advice,new stuff helped me know more about the IT world. Although I consider myself somewhat introverted, I try to make effort to communicate more especially during lunch or outside work hours. Furthermore, since Bahasa Indonesia is the main language used in daily communication, I initially experienced difficulty understanding and  expressing my ideas clearly. Through as time passes by,my conversational skills gradually improved.

5. **Future Career Plan**

Building a strong resume is essential to preparing for future career opportunities. This internship is a valuable addition, demonstrating that real experience has been gained in frontend development from Major Enterprise Systems that will easily land me job opportunities in these client companies. The experience emphasized the importance of strong technical and language preparation before entering a professional environment. It helped determine that this career path aligns perfectly with my personal interests and abilities, enhancing my competitiveness for future job opportunities in the tech industry.

##  {#heading-1}

#  {#heading-1}

# **CHAPTER V RECOMMENDATION** {#heading-1}

##  {#heading-1}

1. **Conclusion**

In conclusion, my internship experience at PT Shibly Technology Solusi has been extremely rewarding and informative, serving as the optimal bridge between my academic background and my future career ambitions. I have gained valuable insights into the crucial role of frontend development in enterprise and telecommunication systems, and how these systems interact with backend services through APIs. The skills, knowledge, and networking achieved during the past months have empowered me to take on the challenges of the modern-day working environment, particularly in handling real-world UAT sessions, learning new frameworks independently, and adapting to fast-paced, multidisciplinary IT projects.

2. **Recommendation for the Institution**

For host institutions, especially those operating in IT and system integration fields, it is recommended to provide a structured technical onboarding program for interns at the beginning of the internship period. Introducing interns to system architecture, development tools, coding standards, and project workflows would enable them to adapt more quickly and work more efficiently. Such onboarding would also help interns better understand their roles and expectations, allowing them to contribute more effectively to ongoing IT projects while reducing the initial learning curve. This approach would benefit both the interns and the organization by improving productivity and collaboration throughout the internship period.

3. **Recommendation for the University**

While the ERP concentration naturally aligns with consulting-based work, the internship experience reinforces the value of project-based learning using real consulting simulations across all Computer Science concentrations, not only ERP. In industry, graduates are expected to analyze problems, design system solutions, and implement them in real organizational contexts, regardless of specialization.The university's current approach in Semester 6, which implements a bootcamp, is a highly effective method tailored to these needs. This intensive, fast-paced program correctly emphasizes a hands-on learning environment with a focus on 75% practice and 25% theory. Through this outcome-oriented approach, students act as consultants for fictitious companies, analyzing business or system problems, designing appropriate solutions, and implementing system components or prototypes.  It is therefore recommended that the university maintain and potentially expand this bootcamp method, as its collaborative and skill-focused structure successfully prepares students from all concentrations to build their professional portfolios, meet industry expectations, and perform more effectively during internships and early professional careers.
